openai.raw:
Independent assessments

Sample A (Image 1)
1) Color and transparency: golden yellow; semi-transparent with mild haze.
2) Coagulation structures: yes – faint, diffuse wispy/veil-like material and subtle clouding in the lower half; no discrete compact clot.
3) Coagulation stage: early_fibrin.
4) Density/homogeneity: slightly inhomogeneous; denser-looking, hazier region toward the bottom.
5) Unique features: subtle internal cloudlets; small dark specks; no wall-adherent compact mass.

Sample B (Image 2)
1) Color and transparency: golden yellow; slightly clearer than A; semi-transparent.
2) Coagulation structures: no obvious clots. One thin thread-like filament along the left wall (could be extraneous); otherwise no visible strands or aggregates.
3) Coagulation stage: none (based on absence of visible fibrin/clumps).
4) Density/homogeneity: most homogeneous of the three; uniform appearance.
5) Unique features: scattered droplets/bubbles on the glass; single hair-/fiber-like filament near wall.

Sample C (Image 3)
1) Color and transparency: golden yellow; semi-transparent overall, but with a localized opaque area.
2) Coagulation structures: yes – a pale, semi-opaque sheet-like mass adherent to the right inner wall extending from near the surface downward; suggests organized coagulum.
3) Coagulation stage: partial_clot.
4) Density/homogeneity: heterogeneous due to the wall-adherent mass; remaining liquid appears relatively uniform.
5) Unique features: broad lamellar/wedge-shaped structure on the wall; slight bottom film.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample C (distinct wall-adherent coagulum).
- Least coagulated: Sample B (no visible fibrin/clots; most homogeneous).
- Visible differences: C shows a clear adherent mass; A shows diffuse early wisps/haze without a compact clot; B appears uniform with no coagulation structures (aside from a possible extraneous filament). Color is similar across all three; transparency varies slightly (B clearer than A; C disrupted by adherent mass).
- Overall visual difference: moderate.

Per-sample assessments (independent):

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Pale straw-yellow; overall semi-transparent (clearer rim, slightly turbid core).
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. A thin translucent rim/skin around the droplet with a few wispy strands across the lumen suggest early fibrin film; no discrete solid clot masses.
- Coagulation stage: early_fibrin.
- Density and homogeneity: Mostly homogeneous liquid core with a slightly more viscous boundary layer; low overall density.
- Unique features: Lens-shaped droplet adhered to tube wall, surrounded by a faint halo; delicate surface film; no cracking or reticulation.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow central area with a broad, faint gray‑green translucent film; overall semi-transparent.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Extensive thin membrane radiating from the center, with polygonal cracking/reticulation and fine linear strands; central mass remains gelatinous.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot.
- Density and homogeneity: Marked gradient—denser yellow center, thinning to a wide peripheral film; heterogeneous with visible network texture.
- Unique features: Pronounced craquelure-like network; radial striations and small rounded pores/voids within the film.

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: Deeper yellow; overall semi-transparent with an opaque central dome.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Thicker central coagulum with a dense, lacy fibrin network extending outward; polygonal “cells” more defined than in Sample B.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (more advanced than Sample B).
- Density and homogeneity: Highest apparent density at the center; heterogeneous but with a broad, thicker peripheral zone compared with B.
- Unique features: Strong yellow ring; well‑defined polygonal compartments with darker round inclusions; dense micro‑branching along the contact line.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample C.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample A.
- Visible differences: A shows mainly a smooth droplet with only a thin surface film; B exhibits a wide, cracked fibrin membrane with a semi‑liquid center; C shows a larger, more opaque central coagulum and a denser, more extensive fibrin network than B.
- Overall visual difference between the samples: Pronounced.

Independent assessment

Sample A (Image 1)
1) Color/transparency: bright/yellow; mostly transparent with slight haze.

Sample B (Image 2)

- Overall visual difference: pronounced.

Per-sample assessments

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color/transparency: Pale yellow; overall semi-transparent with locally opaque areas.
- Clots/fibrin: Yes — a large, cohesive gelatinous mass adherent to the tube wall; peripheral wispy edges suggest a fibrin network.
- Coagulation stage: Full_coagulation (mature, compact clot present with minimal free liquid in the field of view).
- Density/homogeneity: High-density, inhomogeneous; thick consolidated region with variable thickness.
- Unique features: Disc-like/plate-like clot attached to the inner wall; smooth outer contour with faint internal lamination.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color/transparency: Bright yellow; clear/transparent.
- Clots/fibrin: Yes — thin, translucent veil/film and wispy strand along the lower wall and interface; no bulky mass.
- Coagulation stage: Early_fibrin.
- Density/homogeneity: Predominantly homogeneous liquid with a sparse, delicate fibrin film.
- Unique features: Narrow, curling sheet at mid-lower level; faint meniscus-bound layer.

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color/transparency: Bright yellow; mostly transparent.
- Clots/fibrin: Yes — broad, wrinkled sheet-like fibrin layer covering much of the lower half and wall; thicker than in Sample B but not a solid plug.
- Coagulation stage: Partial_clot.
- Density/homogeneity: Moderately heterogeneous; liquid phase plus an extensive, uneven fibrin lamella.
- Unique features: Folded/wrinkled fibrin sheet conforming to the tube curvature with layered appearance.

- Most advanced coagulation: Sample A.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample B.

- Overall visual difference: Pronounced.
